  exact R4 Person qm

axiom Marketing : Prop
axiom MoreCustomers : Prop
axiom SalesUp : Prop
axiom CostsUp : Prop

-- If they invested in marketing, they gained more customers.
axiom invest_imp_customers : Marketing → MoreCustomers
-- With more customers, sales go up.
axiom customers_imp_sales : MoreCustomers → SalesUp
-- Either they focused on marketing, or production costs increased, or both.
axiom disj : Marketing ∨ CostsUp ∨ (Marketing ∧ CostsUp)

theorem costs_increased_of_no_sales : ¬SalesUp → CostsUp := by
  intro hNotS
  have m_imp_s : Marketing → SalesUp := fun hm => customers_imp_sales (invest_imp_customers hm)
  have not_m : ¬Marketing := fun hm => hNotS (m_imp_s hm)
  cases disj with
  | inl hM =>
      have : False := not_m hM
      exact False.elim this
  | inr h =>
      cases h with
      | inl hC => exact hC
      | inr hMC =>
          exact And.right hMC
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

axiom ThrivedPlants : obj → Prop

  have hNotL : ¬ Learned Mary :=
    fun hL => T (hLT hL)
  cases R3 with
  | inl hL => exact False.elim (hNotL hL)
  | inr hS => exact hS
</lean>

axiom T2 : FertilizerAdded

  have hF : FertilizerAdded := T2

  have hE : EnrichedSoil := gardener_enriched_soil
  have hN : SoilNutrientRich := R2 hE
  have hH : HealthyPlants := R3 hN
  exact R4 hH

          exact R1 SamParty g hg
  | inr hncake =>
      have : False := hncake hcake
      exact False.elim this

-- If someone prepared well, then they are ready
axiom A2 : ∀ p : Person, PreparedWell p → Ready p

  have hNotReady : ¬ Ready John := Or.resolve_left hDisj hNotL
  have hPrep : PreparedWell John := A4 John hDil
  have hReady : Ready John := A2 John hPrep
  exact hNotReady hReady

-- from the given axioms.

axiom Fail : obj → Prop

axiom Ban : Prop

-- If someone plants a garden, they will need to water and care for the plants.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, PlantGarden x → (NeedWater x ∧ NeedCare x)
-- If there is a drought, there will be a ban on watering gardens.

-- Either Mark plants a garden, or there is a drought (or both).

axiom R4 : Ban → ¬ Watered Mark
-- If the plants don't get watered, the garden will fail to thrive.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, ¬ Watered x → Fail x

-- Mark did not need to care for garden plants.
axiom Tnot : ¬ NeedCare Mark

theorem mark_garden_fail : Fail Mark := by
  -- From "if plant then need care" and "not need care", deduce "did not plant"
  have h_not_plant : ¬ PlantGarden Mark := by
    intro hp
    have hcare : NeedCare Mark := (R1 Mark hp).right
    exact Tnot hcare
  -- From (Plant Mark ∨ Drought) and ¬Plant Mark, deduce Drought
  have h_drought : Drought := by
    cases R3 with
    | inl hp => exact False.elim (h_not_plant hp)
    | inr hd => exact hd
  -- Chain to ban, then not watered, then fail to thrive
  have h_ban : Ban := R2 h_drought
  have h_not_watered : ¬ Watered Mark := R4 h_ban
  exact R5 Mark h_not_watered
</lean>

          have contra : False := hNotP hP
          exact False.elim contra

<lean>
axiom Rain : Prop
axiom Wet : Prop
axiom WashedCar : Prop
axiom WaterUsedOnCar : Prop
axiom HoseUncoiled : Prop
axiom SpongeSoapy : Prop

-- If the driveway is wet, then it must have rained recently.
axiom R1 : Wet → Rain
-- If someone washed their car, then they used water on the car.
axiom R2 : WashedCar → WaterUsedOnCar
-- Either the driveway is wet, or no water was used on the car, or the driveway is wet even though no water was used.
axiom R3 : Wet ∨ ¬ WaterUsedOnCar ∨ (Wet ∧ ¬ WaterUsedOnCar)
-- If the hose is uncoiled, it means someone was washing their car.
axiom R4 : HoseUncoiled → WashedCar
-- Either the hose is uncoiled, or the sponge is soapy (or both).
axiom R5 : HoseUncoiled ∨ SpongeSoapy

theorem sponge_soapy (hNR : ¬ Rain) : SpongeSoapy := by
  -- From not Rain and Wet → Rain, derive not Wet
  have hNotWet : ¬ Wet := fun hWet => hNR (R1 hWet)
  -- From the trilemma and not Wet, derive no water was used on the car
  have hNotWU : ¬ WaterUsedOnCar := by
    cases R3 with
    | inl hWet =>
        exact fun _ => False.elim (hNotWet hWet)
    | inr h =>
        cases h with
        | inl hNoWater => exact hNoWater
        | inr hAnd => exact hAnd.right
  -- If the hose is uncoiled, then water was used on the car
  have hHoseImpliesWater : HoseUncoiled → WaterUsedOnCar := fun h => R2 (R4 h)
  -- Therefore, the hose is not uncoiled
  have hNotHose : ¬ HoseUncoiled := fun h => hNotWU (hHoseImpliesWater h)
  -- From (HoseUncoiled ∨ SpongeSoapy) and ¬HoseUncoiled, deduce SpongeSoapy
  cases R5 with
  | inl hHose => exact False.elim (hNotHose hHose)
  | inr hSoapy => exact hSoapy
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

axiom Person : Type
axiom Mary : Person

axiom Sale : Prop
axiom Payday : Prop
axiom PricesUp : Prop

axiom StockUp : Person → Prop
axiom MakeList : Person → Prop
axiom Plan : Person → Prop
axiom PlanAhead : Person → Prop

axiom R1 : Sale → ∀ p : Person, StockUp p
axiom R2 : ∀ p : Person, MakeList p → Plan p
axiom D1 : Sale ∨ (∀ p : Person, ¬ Plan p) ∨ (Sale ∧ (∀ p : Person, ¬ PlanAhead p))
axiom R3 : Payday → ∀ p : Person, MakeList p
axiom D2 : Payday ∨ PricesUp ∨ (Payday ∧ PricesUp)

theorem mary_prices_up : (¬ StockUp Mary) → PricesUp := by
  intro hNotStockMary
  -- From not stocking up, deduce not Sale
  have hNotSale : ¬ Sale := by
    intro hSale
    have hAllStock : ∀ p : Person, StockUp p := R1 hSale
    have hMaryStock : StockUp Mary := hAllStock Mary
    exact hNotStockMary hMaryStock
  -- From D1 and not Sale, deduce that people don't plan what to buy
  have hNoPlan : ∀ p : Person, ¬ Plan p := by
    -- D1 is: Sale ∨ (∀ p, ¬ Plan p) ∨ (Sale ∧ (∀ p, ¬ PlanAhead p))
    apply Or.elim D1
    · intro hSale
      exact False.elim (hNotSale hSale)
    · intro hRight
      apply Or.elim hRight
      · intro hNoPlan
        exact hNoPlan
      · intro hSaleAnd
        exact False.elim (hNotSale hSaleAnd.left)
  -- Payday implies everyone plans (via making a list)
  have hPay_implies_plan : Payday → ∀ p : Person, Plan p := by
    intro hPay
    intro p
    have hMake : MakeList p := (R3 hPay) p
    exact R2 p hMake
  -- Therefore, not Payday
  have hNotPayday : ¬ Payday := by
    intro hPay
    have hAllPlan : ∀ p : Person, Plan p := hPay_implies_plan hPay
    have hPlanMary : Plan Mary := hAllPlan Mary
    have hNoPlanMary : ¬ Plan Mary := hNoPlan Mary
    exact hNoPlanMary hPlanMary
  -- Use D2 to conclude PricesUp
  have hPrices : PricesUp := by
    -- D2 is: Payday ∨ PricesUp ∨ (Payday ∧ PricesUp)
    apply Or.elim D2
    · intro hPay
      exact False.elim (hNotPayday hPay)
    · intro hRight
      apply Or.elim hRight
      · intro hUp
        exact hUp
      · intro hPayAndUp
        exact hPayAndUp.right
  exact hPrices
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

  -- From not stocking up, deduce not Sale
  have hNotSale : ¬ Sale := by
    intro hSale
    have hAllStock : ∀ p : Person, StockUp p := R1 hSale
    have hMaryStock : StockUp Mary := hAllStock Mary
    exact hNotStockMary hMaryStock
  -- From D1 and not Sale, deduce that people don't plan what to buy
  have hNoPlan : ∀ p : Person, ¬ Plan p := by
    -- D1 is: Sale ∨ (∀ p, ¬ Plan p) ∨ (Sale ∧ (∀ p, ¬ PlanAhead p))
    apply Or.elim D1
    · intro hSale
      exact False.elim (hNotSale hSale)
    · intro hRight
      apply Or.elim hRight
      · intro hNoPlan
        exact hNoPlan
      · intro hSaleAnd
        exact False.elim (hNotSale hSaleAnd.left)
  -- Payday implies everyone plans (via making a list)
  have hPay_implies_plan : Payday → ∀ p : Person, Plan p := by
    intro hPay
    intro p
    have hMake : MakeList p := (R3 hPay) p
    exact R2 p hMake
  -- Therefore, not Payday
  have hNotPayday : ¬ Payday := by
    intro hPay
    have hAllPlan : ∀ p : Person, Plan p := hPay_implies_plan hPay
    have hPlanMary : Plan Mary := hAllPlan Mary
    have hNoPlanMary : ¬ Plan Mary := hNoPlan Mary
    exact hNoPlanMary hPlanMary
  -- Use D2 to conclude PricesUp
  have hPrices : PricesUp := by
    -- D2 is: Payday ∨ PricesUp ∨ (Payday ∧ PricesUp)
    apply Or.elim D2
    · intro hPay
      exact False.elim (hNotPayday hPay)
    · intro hRight
      apply Or.elim hRight
      · intro hUp
        exact hUp
      · intro hPayAndUp
        exact hPayAndUp.right
  exact hPrices

axiom obj : Type
axiom AddsSalt : obj → Prop
axiom Tastes : obj → Prop

axiom Thickens : Prop

axiom R_addsSalt_savory : ∀ x : obj, AddsSalt x → Savory
axiom R_stirs_thickens : ∀ x : obj, Stirs x → Thickens
axiom Law_salt_or_notthick :
  (∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) ∨ (¬ Thickens ∨ ((∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) ∧ ¬ Thickens))
axiom R_tastes_stirs : ∀ x : obj, Tastes x → Stirs x
axiom Secret_taste_or_salad : ∃ x : obj, Tastes x ∨ Salad x

theorem prepared_salad_from_not_savory : ¬ Savory → ∃ x : obj, Salad x := by
  intro hns
  -- No one added salt
  have hNoSaltAll : ∀ x : obj, ¬ AddsSalt x := by
    intro x
    intro hx
    exact hns (R_addsSalt_savory x hx)
  have hNoSaltExists : ¬ (∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) := by
    intro h
    cases h with
    | intro w hw =>
      exact hNoSaltAll w hw
  -- Hence the stew does not thicken
  have hNotThick : ¬ Thickens := by
    cases Law_salt_or_notthick with
    | inl hSomeSalt =>
      exact False.elim (hNoSaltExists hSomeSalt)
    | inr rest =>
      cases rest with
      | inl hnot => exact hnot
      | inr hboth =>
        have hSomeSalt : ∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x := And.left hboth
        exact False.elim (hNoSaltExists hSomeSalt)
  -- Therefore no one stirs, and so no one tastes
  have hNoStirs : ∀ x : obj, ¬ Stirs x := by
    intro x
    intro hs
    exact hNotThick (R_stirs_thickens x hs)
  have hNoTastes : ∀ x : obj, ¬ Tastes x := by
    intro x
    intro ht
    exact hNoStirs x (R_tastes_stirs x ht)
  -- From the secret, deduce someone prepared a salad
  cases Secret_taste_or_salad with
  | intro w hdisj =>
    cases hdisj with
    | inl ht =>
      exact False.elim (hNoTastes w ht)
    | inr hs =>
      exact Exists.intro w hs
</lean>

axiom Sam : Person

axiom KitchenHot : Prop
axiom StoveOn : Prop

axiom Hungry : Person → Prop
axiom MakePancakes : Person → Prop
axiom UseStove : Person → Prop
axiom AteDinner : Person → Prop

-- If the stove is on, the kitchen gets hot
axiom R_on_hot : StoveOn → KitchenHot
-- If someone makes pancakes, they use the stove
axiom R_make_use : ∀ x : Person, MakePancakes x → UseStove x
-- Either the stove was on, or Sam did not use the stove
axiom R_on_or_notuse : StoveOn ∨ ¬ UseStove Sam
-- If someone is hungry, they make pancakes
axiom R_hungry_make : ∀ x : Person, Hungry x → MakePancakes x
-- If Sam did not eat dinner last night, he was hungry this morning
axiom R_notEat_hungry : ¬ AteDinner Sam → Hungry Sam

open Classical

theorem sam_ate_from_not_hot (nh : ¬ KitchenHot) : AteDinner Sam :=
by
  by_contra hNotEat
  have hHungry : Hungry Sam := R_notEat_hungry hNotEat
  have hMake : MakePancakes Sam := R_hungry_make Sam hHungry
  have hUse : UseStove Sam := R_make_use Sam hMake
  have hOn : StoveOn :=
    by
      cases R_on_or_notuse with
      | inl hon => exact hon
      | inr hnotuse =>
        exact False.elim (hnotuse hUse)
  have hHot : KitchenHot := R_on_hot hOn
  exact nh hHot
</lean>

    cases disj with

    intro hF
    have hC : ClearMind Alex := R1 Alex hF
    exact hNC hC

    intro hO
    have hF : Finish Alex := R2 Alex hO
    exact hNF hF
  -- ¬Todo Alex (contrapose Todo → Organized)
  intro hT
  have hO : Organized Alex := R3 Alex hT
  exact hNO hO

    (fun hNotPost => False.elim (hNotPost hPost))
  have hPrepared : Prepared := R1 hPractice
  have hPlay : PlayWell := R4 hPrepared
  exact R5 hPlay
